title: Methods and systems for locating and treating with cold therapy
abstract: The present invention generally relates to improved medical devices, systems, and methods. In many embodiments, devices, systems, and methods for locating and treating a target nerve with cold therapy are provided. For example, a focused cold therapy treatment device may be provided that is adapted to couple with or be fully integrated with a nerve stimulation device such that nerve stimulation and focused cold therapy may be performed concurrently with the cryo-stimulation device. Improvements in nerve localization and targeting may increase treatment accuracy and physician confidence in needle placement during treatment. In turn, such improvements may decrease overall treatment times, the number of repeat treatments, and the re-treatment rate. Further, additional improvements in nerve localization and targeting may reduce the number of applied treatment cycles and may also reduce the number of cartridge changes (when replaceable refrigerant cartridges are used.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  Whaj ig claimed ig; 1. A ciy o-stimulation treatment device, comprising: a needle having a proximal end and a distal end and a length therebetween, the needle configured to produce a cold zone for focused cold therapy, the needle having a cooling center along the length of the needle that is associated with a center of the cold zone produced by the needle; an electrically insulated coating disposed about the length of the needle; wherein the needle is electrically conductive and wherein the proximal end of the needle is configured to couple with an electrical nerve stimulation generator that generates an electrical field about the distal end of the needle for electrically stimulating and locating the target nerve; and wherein the cooling center of the needle is uninsulated such that an intensity of the electrical field is co-incident with the center of the cold zone produced by the needle. 2. The treatment device of claim 1, wherein the electrical nerve stimulation generator couples with an uninsulated portion of the proximal end of the needle. 3. The treatment device of claim 1 , further comprising a handle defined by a housing, and wherein the housing houses the electrical nerve stimulation generator. 4. The treatment device of claim 1 , further comprising a handle defined by a housing, and wherein the housing incorporates an electrical port that electrically couples with an uninsulated portion of the proximal end of the needle, the electrical port configured to receive an input associated with the electrical nerve stimulation generator to electrically couple the electrical nerve stimulation generator and the needle. 5. The treatment device of claim 1 , wherein the needle is part of a replaceable needle assembly configured for releaseable attachment to a handpiece, and wherein the replaceable needle assembly comprises an electrical port that electrically couples with an uninsulated portion of the proximal end of the needle, the electrical port configured to receive an input associated with the waveform generator to electrically couple the electrical nerve stimulation generator and the needle. 6. The treatment device of claim 1 , wherein the electrically insulated coating comprises a fluoropolymer coating. 7. The treatment device of claim 1, wherein the electrically insulated coating comprises a silicone rubber coating. 8. The treatment device of claim 1 , wherein the electrically insulated coating comprises a polyimide coating. 9. The treatment device of claim 1 , wherein the electrically insulated coating comprises a parylene coating. 10. The treatment device of claim 1, wherein the electrically insulated coating comprises an epoxy coating. 11. The treatment device of claim 1 , wherein the electrically insulated coating comprises a ceramic coating. 12. The treatment device of claim 1 , wherein the needle is a first needle of a needle assembly having the first needle and a second needle adjacent the first needle, and wherein the second needle acts as an electrical ground during electrical stimulation of the nerve by the first needle. 13. A cryo-stimulation treatment device, comprising: a needle assembly having: one or more treatment needles configured to produce a cold zone for focused cold therapy of a target nerve; and one or more stimulation needles constructed of electrically conductive material and being configured to couple with an electrical nerve stimulation generator to produce an electrical field for stimulating the target nerve; and an electrically insulating coating on the one or more stimulation needles; wherein the one or more stimulation needles are uninsulated by the electrically insulating coating at a location of the one or more stimulation needles that is coincident with a center of the cold zone produced by the one or more treatment needles. 14. The treatment device of claim 13, wherein the one or more stimulation needles comprises a center needle and wherein the one or more treatment needles comprises at least two needles that are adjacent the center needle and on opposite sides of the center needle. 15. The treatment device of claim 13, wherein the one or more treatment needles comprises an electrically insulating coating and wherein at least a distal portion of the one or more treatment needles is uninsulated and acts as an electrical ground during electrical stimulation of the target nerve by the one or more stimulation needles. 16. The treatment device of claim 13, wherein the one or more treatment needles are also stimulation needles constructed of electrically conductive material and being configured to couple with an electrical nerve stimulation generator to produce an electrical field for stimulating the target nerve. 17. The treatment device of claim 13, further comprising a handle defined by a housing, and wherein a distal end of the housing includes an electrical adapter that electrically couples the needle assembly to the handle; and wherein the housing houses the electrical nerve stimulation generator and connection of the needle assembly to the adapter electrically couples the one or more stimulation needles with the electrical nerve stimulation generator. 18. The treatment device of claim 13, further comprising a handle defined by a housing, and wherein the housing incorporates an electrical port mat electrically couples with an uninsulated portion of the one or more stimulation needles, the electrical port configured to receive an input associated with the electrical nerve stimulation generator to electrically couple the electrical nerve stimulation generator and the one or more stimulation needles. 19. The treatment device of claim 13, wherein the needle assembly is configured for releaseable attachment to a handpiece, and wherein the replaceable needle assembly comprises an electrical port that electrically couples with an uninsulated portion of the one or more stimulating needles, the electrical port configured to receive an input associated with the electrical nerve stimulation generator to electrically couple the electrical nerve stimulation generator and the needle. 20. A method of treating a nerve, the method comprising: inserting one or more needles of a needle assembly into a tissue of a patient; electrically stimulating the nerve with the needle assembly to localize the nerve within the tissue; after localizing of the nerve, delivering a focused cold therapy to the nerve with the needle assembly; and during delivery of the focused cold therapy, electrically stimulating the nerve with the needle assembly; sensing an activity of the nerve during the delivery of the focused cold therapy for feedback on the delivery of the focused cold therapy to the nerve. 21. The method of claim 20, further comprising coupling an input associated with an electrical nerve stimulation generator to an electrical port to electrically couple the electrical nerve stimulation generator and the needle assembly. 22. The method of claim 21, wherein coupling the input associated with the electrical nerve stimulation generator to the electrical port comprises coupling the input associated with the electrical nerve stimulation generator to an electrical port of the needle assembly. 23. The method of claim 21, wherein coupling the input associated with the electrical nerve stimulation generator to the electrical port comprises coupling the input associated with the electrical nerve stimulation generator to an electrical port disposed on a handle supporting the needle assembly. 24. The method of claim 20, further comprising coupling the needle assembly to an adapter of a treatment device handle, and wherein coupling the needle assembly to the adapter of the treatment device handle electrically couples an electrical nerve stimulation generator housed in the treatment device handle to one or more needles of the needle assembly. 25. The method of claim 20, wherein the needle assembly electrically stimulates the nerve and delivers the focused cold therapy with the same needle. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein the needle of the needle assembly that electrically stimulates the nerve and delivers the focused cold therapy has a length between 5-20 cm and a blunt distal tip. 27. The method of claim 20, wherein the needle assembly electrically stimulates the nerve and delivers the focused cold therapy with different needles of the needle assembly. 28. The method of claim 20, wherein the needle assembly electrically stimulates the nerve and delivers the focused cold therapy with different needles of the needle assembly. 29. The method of claim 20, wherein the needle assembly includes at least a first needle and a second needle adjacent the first needle, and wherein the first needle electrically stimulates the target nerve and wherein the second needle acts as an electrical ground during electrical stimulation of the nerve by the first needle. 30. The method of claim 20, wherein the needle assembly includes a center needle and needles adjacent to the center needle, and wherein electrically stimulating the 

 target nerve is performed with the center needle and wherein delivering the focused cold therapy is performed with the needles adjacent to the center needle. 31. A cooling treatment device, comprising: a first needle having a proximal end and a distal end and a length therebetween; a second needle having a proximal end and a distal end and a length therebetween; wherein the first needle is electrically conductive and wherein the proximal end of the first needle is coupled with an electrical nerve stimulation generator that generates an electrical field about the distal end of the first needle for nerve stimulation; and wherein the second needle acts as an electrical ground during nerve stimulation; and wherein at least one of the first needle and the second needle is configured to produce a cold zone for focused cold therapy.
